question: Does the NORMALIZED sibling separation PLATEAU (OSC-consistent) or DECAY (OSC-fails)?
resolution: TWO distinct separations were being conflated. Corrected normalization (ROOT/map separation) PLATEAUS at a positive floor; the DECAYING 0.333->0.111 was the point-set/boundary gap, which is OSC-permitted (closures may touch).

lattice_floor_minMg_over_v_in_pm3.note: min|M^g v|_inf stays in [0.68,1.0]*3^g -> NEVER decays relative to 3^g (quasi-similarity).

exact_Q_metric_plateau.consequence: |M^g v|_Q = 3^g |v|_Q EXACTLY. Root sep of any two distinct gen-g cyls = 3^g * |a-a'|_Q >= 3^g * s_min. Gen-g cyl Q-diameter = 3^g * D_seed. Hence ROOT-sep / diam = s_min/D_seed = CONSTANT in g -> EXACT PLATEAU, positive floor, scale-invariant.

ROOT_map_separation.what: min |root_a - root_b| over distinct equal-gen cyls; root = M^g * ancestor. This is the Bandt-Graf neighbor-MAP quantity (id in closure of neighbor set).
ROOT_map_separation.trend: PLATEAU at positive floor ~0.28-0.29 (Cheb) / exact constant (Q)
ROOT_map_separation.verdict_contribution: OSC-CONSISTENT: neighbor maps with distinct roots stay bounded away from identity.

POINTSET_gap.what: min point-set Cheb distance over sibling cyls (closest twig/refill approach).
POINTSET_gap.trend: DECAYS as 3^-(g-1); numerator PINNED at lattice quantum 1.
POINTSET_gap.verdict_contribution: Boundary-contact only. OSC PERMITS closures to touch on measure-zero sets. NOT an OSC violation by itself.

prior_0.333_0.111_reexamined.why_it_looked_like_OSC_fails: It used the POINT-SET/boundary gap (wrong quantity) as numerator; that gap is pinned at the lattice quantum 1, so dividing by a 3^g-growing scale manufactures a spurious 3^-(g-1) decay.
prior_0.333_0.111_reexamined.correct_quantity: ROOT/map separation -> plateaus positive; boundary touching is OSC-allowed.

adversarial_routing.root_sep_router_independent: root = M^g*(integer anchor); root-sep >= 3^g*s_min for ANY legal router (linear-algebra fact, M invertible + MtQM=9Q). No adversarial legal router can push normalized root separation below the deep-tail/packing floor. Point-set gap >= 1 for any router (distinct points, disjoint-union 0 duplicates).

covering_multiplicity_bounded.means: WSC holds; no positive-fraction overlap (would flood).

what_is_NOT_certified.reason: The JOINT (Delta,sigma,sigma) neighbor automaton under JOINT legality was NOT built/closed (78728^2 side-state product infeasible in budget). Only the prior DECOUPLED (sigma-ignored) automaton exists; it reached Delta=0 (word-address map-coincidence f_v=f_w, v!=w) but that is the sigma-ignored FLOODING artifact and is not trustworthy.
what_is_NOT_certified.open_OSC_gap: Finite type => OSC <=> id not in neighbor set <=> no two DISTINCT jointly-legal words yield the same map (Delta=0). Realized orbit has 0 coincidences (disjoint union). Whether a Delta=0 word-address coincidence is reachable under JOINT legality is UNRESOLVED.

verdict.scaling_answer: PLATEAU (OSC-consistent) on the correct (root/map) normalization; the DECAY is the boundary-contact artifact of the wrong (point-set) normalization.
verdict.reverses_prior_round: Yes -- last round read the 0.333->0.111 point-set decay as 'OSC fails'; corrected, the OSC-deciding root separation plateaus positive (exactly, via MtQM=9Q).
verdict.certified_floor: WSC (bounded multiplicity, robust under adversarial routing).
verdict.OSC_status: OSC-CONSISTENT but NOT CERTIFIED -- sole remaining gap is the joint-legality Delta=0 map-coincidence check (joint automaton unbuilt).
